name: Consolidated
description_short: Consolidated Communications (now Fidium) is a telecommunications provider offering residential and business internet, phone, and streaming TV services across multiple regions.
description_long: Consolidated Communications is a regional telecommunications company that provides broadband internet, voice phone services, and streaming television to residential and business customers. The company has undergone a rebranding initiative, now operating under the Fidium brand for consumer-facing services. Based on their website, they focus on delivering connectivity solutions to areas across the United States, with particular emphasis on fiber expansion into underserved markets. The company offers tiered residential services including high-speed and fiber internet (up to 1 Gig speeds), traditional landline and VoIP phone options, and bundled streaming TV packages featuring partners like ESPN, HGTV, and HBO. For business customers, Consolidated provides scalable solutions ranging from small business packages (1-50 employees) through medium and enterprise offerings that include data networking, voice services, data center solutions, managed IT services, and cloud infrastructure. They also operate wholesale carrier services for telecommunications partners. Consolidated distinguishes itself through geographic service breadth, offering connectivity in areas that larger national carriers may not prioritize, and through their investment in fiber infrastructure expansion. They provide industry-specific solutions for government, education, retail, real estate, construction, financial services, healthcare, manufacturing, and call centers. The company maintains dedicated support channels for different customer segments and offers customer assistance programs including Lifeline services for eligible low-income households. However, it's important to note that Consolidated Communications operates as a traditional utility telecommunications company, not as a consumer finance provider. Their placement in the "payday-alternatives" category appears to be a significant miscategorization error. While they do offer residential service packages at different price points, they are fundamentally a broadband and phone service provider, not a financial services company offering loans, credit products, or alternative lending solutions. Consumers seeking actual financial services should look elsewhere.
services: Residential high-speed and fiber internet (up to 1 Gig speeds)
VoIP and traditional landline phone services
Streaming TV bundles with third-party partners
Small business internet and phone packages
Medium and enterprise data networking services
Wholesale digital infrastructure for carrier partners
Industry-specific solutions (government, education, healthcare, financial services, etc.)
Lifeline and assistance programs for eligible customers
pros: Offers fiber internet expansion with speeds up to 1 Gig for areas traditionally underserved by major providers
Provides bundled packages combining internet, phone, and streaming TV with flexibility to customize services
Includes Lifeline and assistance programs specifically for low-income residential customers
Maintains dedicated support infrastructure for both residential and business customers with specialized resources
Offers industry-specific solutions tailored to government, education, healthcare, retail, and other sectors
Provides wholesale carrier services and network infrastructure for other telecommunications partners
Available account management through online portals (MyCCI for small business, Account Center for enterprise, VFO for wholesale)
cons: Service availability varies significantly by location and ZIP code—not a nationwide provider
Company rebranding to Fidium may create confusion during transition period
Streaming TV offerings are dependent on third-party partnerships rather than proprietary content
No indication of competitive pricing transparency directly on website—requires location lookup to view plans
As a regional provider, may lack the 24/7 support infrastructure of national telecommunications carriers
best_for: Residential customers in rural or underserved areas seeking high-speed fiber internet and bundled services
Small businesses (1-50 employees) needing bundled phone, internet, and managed IT solutions
Medium to enterprise organizations requiring comprehensive data networking and cloud services
Low-income households eligible for Lifeline and assistance programs seeking affordable connectivity
diagnosis: Consolidated Communications is a regional telecommunications utility provider, not a consumer finance company. This listing is fundamentally miscategorized—the company provides internet, phone, and TV services, not loans, credit products, or payday alternatives. Consumers seeking financial services should disregard this profile and look for actual lenders or financial institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
